description_long: This object is a large, wall-mounted array of utility control meters, likely for gas or water, set into a concrete or plaster wall. The front face features a row of round, glass-covered dials with metallic housings, each connected to a vertical pipe or conduit below. The structure is embedded in a rectangular section of wall, with visible wiring and piping on the lower right side, and some surface wear and discoloration. The meters are arranged in a single horizontal row, each with a visible gauge and mechanical fittings. The overall appearance is industrial, with a utilitarian design and exposed mechanical components.
description: A wall-mounted array of industrial utility meters with glass dials and metal housings, set into a concrete wall with visible pipes and wiring.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the thin profile of the wall and meters, with little detail visible.
description_view_1: A frontal view displaying the row of round meters, their glass faces, and the connecting pipes and wiring beneath.
description_view_2: The opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and edge of the wall structure.
description_view_3: A slightly angled frontal view, revealing the full row of meters, their housings, and the supporting wall section.
